page: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-8 is a general-purpose server instance powered by the ARMv8 architecture. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores of an ARM Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or bundled GPUs, and it offers zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results position this instance in the average tier for most multi-core and memory operations, though single-core performance is relatively weak. The Neoverse-N1 processor achieves a stress-ng single-core score of 1812.89 and a multi-core score of 14472.4. Memory read bandwidth is measured at 563.91 GB/sec for cached small blocks and drops to 87.42 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, while write bandwidth is 47.24 GB/sec. The instance demonstrates average performance in software compilation and database operations, alongside a static web serving throughput of 10.09 GB/sec. However, it shows poor performance in LLM text generation and weak single-core Geekbench scores.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the t2a-standard-8 offers a resource-dense ARM-based architecture that provides dedicated compute resources without the overhead of hyperthreading. This makes it a viable option for workloads that benefit from physical core isolation and predictable multi-threaded execution. Given its balanced memory-to-CPU ratio and average performance in database operations and web serving, this instance is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, multi-threaded decompression tasks, and software compilation pipelines.
description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-8 is an ARM-based general-purpose server instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz with 8 physical cores and no hyperthreading, it delivers balanced compute resources with 4.0 GB of memory per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 14472.4 and a static web serving throughput of 10.09 GB/sec, though single-core performance and LLM inference speeds are weak. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for multi-threaded workloads that benefit from dedicated physical cores. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, software compilation, static web serving, and moderate database operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-8 is an ARM-based general-purpose instance with 8 dedicated Neoverse-N1 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and average multi-core performance for web serving and compilation.
